evidence: Both report Chinese text becoming garbled in VS Code; A specifically mentions copying Chinese characters in the terminal turns into乱码, while B says all Chinese text in VS Code is garbled after reinstall/opening. Same symptom and language-specific corruption, likely the same underlying encoding/display issue.

Both report a 1.123.0 regression in the integrated terminal’s OSC 52 clipboard path where base64-decoded UTF-8 bytes are treated as Latin-1, corrupting non-ASCII text on paste; A explicitly blames `@xterm/addon-clipboard` `atob`/`writeText`, and B describes the same multi-byte UTF-8 OSC 52 garbling with Chinese characters.

Both reports are about Linux sandbox/read behavior: ISSUE A says `cat ~/.bashrc` is run twice in the sandbox before failing, and ISSUE B says the sandbox does not detect that a read was blocked while verifying the same area. Both point to the same blocked-read/sandbox handling bug.

Both reports show the agent failing to produce a response on a remote Linux system: A says it 'cannot finish the complete response' and 'plan agent cannot work,' while B says 'Sorry, no response was returned.' The core symptom is a missing/incomplete agent response, differing mainly in wording and OS version.

Both report Copilot access blocked by credit limit despite the user believing they still have credits: A says in Portuguese 'tenho creditos e nao consigo ussar' (I have credits and can’t use it), B explicitly says 'You've reached your monthly credit limit.'

Both report Copilot quota/usage-limit errors despite no actual usage, with the quota incorrectly shown as reached at the start of a month; A mentions inline suggestions quota for March, B shows the same quota-reached popup on first use and notes no monthly limit set.
Both reports say the feature in VS Code is not working and produces no output/replies for the prompt, with no additional distinguishing details.
Both reports say the same thing in different words: the tool/program runs but produces no output despite expecting code analysis/update output.
Both reports describe the same vague failure mode: the feature is completely unresponsive / not working and produces no output or response. No differing symptoms or context are provided.
Both reports are extremely generic and describe the same symptom: code is not running / no output despite expecting it to work. No distinguishing details are given in either issue.

Both reports describe Copilot/analysis jobs appearing to run but never producing any output or answer: A says "analyze code and update" with "no output", and B says it "says working" but provides no answer even after hours.

Both reports describe Copilot Chat being interrupted by GitHub API rate-limit exhaustion / "rate_limited" errors after only a small number of prompts, with recovery after waiting. Issue B adds that it may be triggered by content exclusion rules in a large repo, but the core failure is the same API-limit block.

Both reports describe Copilot Chat sessions being interrupted by GitHub API rate-limit exhaustion in VS Code during normal use; B adds that large repos/AOSP and content-exclusion-rule refreshes may trigger it, but the core failure is the same aggressive rate limiting / repeated API exhaustion.

Both reports are effectively empty placeholders with the same generic structure ('VS Code Version', 'OS Version', steps 1/2) and no distinguishing bug details, so they cannot be distinguished as separate underlying issues.

Both report Copilot/Autopilot treating its own assistant/model turns as if they were user input, causing the chat flow to ignore the human’s expected interjection/wait state. A says it is “confusing it's own thoughts with my interjections”; B says Agent/Plan modes “refuse to wait for answers” and immediately proceed as if no user response is needed.

Both report paid GitHub Copilot users being blocked by premature rate/quota limits despite having used only a small fraction of their monthly allowance; one mentions less than 10% usage and the other says access is lost by day four with only a fraction of 300 prompts used.

Both report a premium/quota/rate-limit block despite the user believing they should still have access (A: "rate limit error" with billed premium requests; B: "premium model quota" message even though I'm on auto). The core issue is incorrect quota enforcement/status.

Both report an incorrect usage/quota exhaustion message despite the user having already increased or paid for higher limits; A says the app shows a rate limit error even with billed premium requests, and B says additional usage limit is reached even though the limit was increased well above threshold.

Both report an incorrect premium/rate-limit/quota block despite having access entitlement and using Auto/Premium requests; Issue B quotes the same type of quota message and Issue A says the app gives a rate limit error even though premium requests are billed.

Both report being blocked by an incorrect usage/rate-limit message despite the user having increased/premium allowance: A says 'rate limit error' even with 'Billied Premium requests,' and B says 'You've reached your additional usage limit' even though the limit was increased well above the threshold.
Both report chat history/session not being persisted. A says "chat not being saved" and B explicitly says the chat session cannot be serialized for storage and will be lost on close.
Both reports say Claude is not working; Issue B adds a specific failure mode ('Claude Code process exited with code 1') that fits the same underlying problem rather than a different feature area.
Both reports describe Claude not working in the same way: the app spins/searches after a prompt and does nothing. Issue A is just a terse summary of the same symptom described in Issue B.
Both reports concern Claude in VS Code failing to function/show content at all, with no distinct symptoms beyond general non-working behavior.

Both reports say Claude is not working in VS Code; A is a general "Claude is not working" report and B specifically says Claude is not loading chat in the window, with no other distinguishing details.

Both report the same core failure message: "Summarization request failed" with a TSX element reference (N3 vs x5) during compact/summarization. Issue B explicitly says `/compact` failed because of that summarization error, matching A's generic failure report.
Both report a misleading/incorrect balance or credit warning: A says they have positive balance but cannot use it, and B says it shows the wrong message about reaching credit when it is not true.

Both reports describe a paid/positive balance or Pro entitlement that the user cannot actually use. A is in Portuguese: "Tenho saldo e não posso usar"; B says "I just paid for Pro version but I can't use it."
Both reports concern Claude failing to execute/respond: A shows repeated "Error during execution" in Claude, and B says Claude keeps spinning/searching and does nothing when any prompt is sent.

Both report the identical runtime failure text, "Error: Error during execution," from the Claude/Claude Code extension in VS Code. Issue B adds the specific context that it happens in the new Agent Window while the classic sidebar works, which matches the same symptom in Issue A.

Both reports involve Claude in the AHP/provider flow throwing the same generic "Error during execution" when AI customizations/session view are used; issue B gives the reproduction path and issue A shows the resulting error text.

Both reports describe Copilot being blocked by a usage/rate-limit-style restriction despite the user expecting access to continue; A says they are repeatedly rate-limited for 30+ minutes, B says Copilot shows it cannot accrue additional premium requests even though the credit budget is not exhausted.

Both reports are essentially the same generic Copilot Chat failure template with no differing symptoms; Issue A says "terminal freeze , no responses" and Issue B appears to be an empty/placeholder issue body with the same metadata fields and no distinct behavior described.
Both report the model unexpectedly switching to "Auto"; A says the model automatically changes and throws an error, and B says every new workspace defaults to Auto for new AH sessions.

Both reports are about Copilot chat model selection failing and producing model-related errors: A says the model automatically changes and throws an error; B says the user cannot select models and gets a 400/error "model is not supported".

Both reports describe unexpectedly high/early request consumption in Copilot Agent Mode despite normal/small usage: A says rate limited after <10 requests, B says premium requests were consumed far faster than expected even though models show 1x multiplier. The core symptom is excessive request usage/rate limiting in Agent Mode.

Both reports say GitHub Copilot Pro is already purchased/available but VS Code keeps prompting to upgrade and blocking normal use/chat input; A says it has shown the upgrade prompt for 5 days, B says Copilot Pro isn’t updating and asks to upgrade in the chatbox.
Both issues report inability to upgrade to GitHub Copilot Pro / seeing an upgrade error for Copilot Pro, with the same core problem phrased in two ways.

Both report GitHub Copilot Pro upgrade/status not taking effect: A says it keeps showing an upgrade-to-Pro error for 5 days, B says after upgrading to Pro it still says monthly credit limit reached and asks to upgrade.

Both reports say Copilot Pro is already purchased but VS Code/Copilot keeps prompting to upgrade and does not unlock chat/input. Issue A says it has shown an upgrade error for 5 days; Issue B says Copilot Pro was bought but chatbox still asks to upgrade.
Both report the same Copilot Pro upgrade problem: A says it shows an 'upgrade to github copilot pro' error and has persisted for days; B explicitly says 'Unable to upgrade copilot pro.'

Both reports describe GitHub Copilot prompting for an upgrade to Copilot Pro despite the user already having Pro or expecting Pro access; the core symptom is the same upgrade/premium entitlements not being recognized.
Both report Copilot Pro entitlement not being recognized and the UI still prompting to upgrade despite already having/using Copilot Pro.

Both issues report inability to upgrade to GitHub Copilot Pro; A mentions seeing an "upgrade to github copilot pro" error/prompt for 5 days, and B says "Unable to upgrade copilot pro" with the same wording.

Both report GitHub Copilot prompting for an upgrade to Copilot Pro / monthly credit limit despite the user already having Pro or expecting access; the core failure is entitlement/status not updating after upgrade.
Both report the assistant failing to answer prompts at all; A says it cannot help with the prompt / gives a refusal, while B says there is no response at all when prompted.

Both report the assistant/prompting failing with essentially the same user-facing message: Issue A says 'Cannot help with that' and Issue B says 'Language model unavailable' when trying to prompt. Both indicate the model/assistant is unavailable rather than different features.
